(formerly the **Brampton Centre for Sports & Entertainment** and the **Powerade Centre**)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a 5,000-seat multi-purpose [arena](/wiki/Arena "Arena") in [Brampton, Ontario](/wiki/Brampton,_Ontario "Brampton, Ontario"), Canada. It was built in 1998, and officially opened the same year on October 7. In 2023, the arena became home to the [Brampton Honey Badgers](/wiki/Brampton_Honey_Badgers "Brampton Honey Badgers") of the [Canadian Elite Basketball League](/wiki/Canadian_Elite_Basketball_League "Canadian Elite Basketball League").[[2]](#cite_note-2) In April 2023, it hosted the [2023 IIHF Women's World Championship](/wiki/2023_IIHF_Women%27s_World_Championship "2023 IIHF Women's World Championship"). It is also home to the new tenants, the [Brampton Steelheads](/wiki/Brampton_Steelheads "Brampton Steelheads") of the Ontario Hockey League as of May 2024. It was previously home to the [Brampton Battalion](/wiki/Brampton_Battalion "Brampton Battalion") of the [Ontario Hockey League](/wiki/Ontario_Hockey_League "Ontario Hockey League"), the [Brampton Beast](/wiki/Brampton_Beast "Brampton Beast") of the [ECHL](/wiki/ECHL "ECHL"), and the [Brampton Excelsiors](/wiki/Brampton_Excelsiors_(MSL) "Brampton Excelsiors (MSL)") lacrosse teams.

which hosted the [Global T20 Canada](/wiki/Global_T20_Canada "Global T20 Canada") tournament from 2019-2024,[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) and is scheduled to host the [Canada Super60 League](/w/index.php?title=Canada_Super60_League&action=edit&redlink=1 "Canada Super60 League (page does not exist)") starting in 2025.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) The city has proposed to construct a new 20,000 seat cricket stadium for $49 million on the location.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Brampton Centre for Sports and Entertainment](/w/index.php?title=Brampton_Centre_for_Sports_and_Entertainment&redirect=no "Brampton Centre for Sports and Entertainment"))

Arena in Brampton, Ontario, Canada

Not to be confused with [CAA Arena](/wiki/CAA_Arena "CAA Arena").

CAA Centre

The **CAA Centre** (formerly the **Brampton Centre for Sports & Entertainment** and the **Powerade Centre**)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a 5,000-seat multi-purpose [arena](/wiki/Arena "Arena") in [Brampton, Ontario](/wiki/Brampton,_Ontario "Brampton, Ontario"), Canada. It was built in 1998, and officially opened the same year on October 7. In 2023, the arena became home to the [Brampton Honey Badgers](/wiki/Brampton_Honey_Badgers "Brampton Honey Badgers") of the [Canadian Elite Basketball League](/wiki/Canadian_Elite_Basketball_League "Canadian Elite Basketball League").[[2]](#cite_note-2) In April 2023, it hosted the [2023 IIHF Women's World Championship](/wiki/2023_IIHF_Women%27s_World_Championship "2023 IIHF Women's World Championship"). It is also home to the new tenants, the [Brampton Steelheads](/wiki/Brampton_Steelheads "Brampton Steelheads") of the Ontario Hockey League as of May 2024. It was previously home to the [Brampton Battalion](/wiki/Brampton_Battalion "Brampton Battalion") of the [Ontario Hockey League](/wiki/Ontario_Hockey_League "Ontario Hockey League"), the [Brampton Beast](/wiki/Brampton_Beast "Brampton Beast") of the [ECHL](/wiki/ECHL "ECHL"), and the [Brampton Excelsiors](/wiki/Brampton_Excelsiors_(MSL) "Brampton Excelsiors (MSL)") lacrosse teams.

In the main arena, the seats are purple, with private suites located around the top of seating area. The club seats are on the penalty box side of the arena. There is a video scoreboard that was added for the Brampton Beast's inaugural season. The concourse is horseshoe-shaped. It is located at 7575 [Kennedy Road](/wiki/List_of_roads_in_Brampton#North–south_roads "List of roads in Brampton"), on the south side of the city, between [Steeles Avenue](/wiki/Steeles_Avenue "Steeles Avenue") and [Highway 407](/wiki/Ontario_Highway_407 "Ontario Highway 407").

The main arena is part of the Brampton Sports Complex that includes three smaller ice pads and outdoor softball diamonds, as well as the **TD Cricket Arena** which hosted the [Global T20 Canada](/wiki/Global_T20_Canada "Global T20 Canada") tournament from 2019-2024,[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) and is scheduled to host the [Canada Super60 League](/w/index.php?title=Canada_Super60_League&action=edit&redlink=1 "Canada Super60 League (page does not exist)") starting in 2025.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) The city has proposed to construct a new 20,000 seat cricket stadium for $49 million on the location.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)
